title: daemon relay_event_loop: no MLS group state reset on Reconnecting — stale epoch causes all BROADCAST decryption failures after reconnect
description: In daemon/src/relay.rs, relay_event_loop handles ClientEvent::Reconnecting only by broadcasting a ConnectionStateChanged event. It does NOT reset the Arc<Mutex<MlsClient>>. After reconnect, the relay creates a fresh session: a new admin creates a new group at epoch 0 and sends a new Welcome via WHISPER. The daemon's MLS client still holds the previous group at some epoch N. When the Welcome arrives, join_from_welcome_for_group returns Err('group already exists') because the group key (GROUP_ID) is the same. The daemon never joins the new session — every subsequent BROADCAST fails MLS decryption silently. CLI (commands.rs line 491) correctly sets mls_active=false on Reconnecting. The daemon must do the same: clear the group from mls_client.groups on Reconnecting.
